features: TL-UL CSR read/write access with byte/word alignment checks
64-bit mtime free-running counter readback via LOW/HIGH registers
12-bit prescaler behavior including zero-prescale and delayed effect on update
Two 64-bit timer comparators with interrupt generation and W1C clearing
Interrupt enable masking and INTR_TEST software injection
Watchdog enable, bark threshold, bite threshold, pet, and count readback
Watchdog lock behavior preventing control changes until reset
Alert output assertion on watchdog bite threshold
Reset initialization and post-reset register defaults

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_and_csr_map
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values for all implemented CSRs, basic TL-UL reads/writes, address decoding across the 64-byte window, and reserved-bit RAZ/WI behavior.

directed_tests.name: mtime_monotonic_and_prescaler_zero
directed_tests.intent: Confirm mtime increments every clock when PRESCALER=0, remains monotonic, and low/high halves read consistently across rollover-sensitive sampling.

directed_tests.name: prescaler_basic_and_update_latency
directed_tests.intent: Program a nonzero prescaler and verify mtime increments once per prescaler+1 cycles; change prescaler while active and confirm the new value takes effect only on the next boundary.

directed_tests.name: timer0_compare_interrupt_flow
directed_tests.intent: Program MTIMECMP0 below/above current mtime, verify raw interrupt assertion, INTR_ENABLE masking, and W1C clearing through INTR_STATE.

directed_tests.name: timer1_compare_interrupt_flow
directed_tests.intent: Repeat comparator interrupt validation for MTIMECMP1 to ensure both comparators operate independently.

directed_tests.name: 64bit_compare_partial_write_intermediate_state
directed_tests.intent: Write comparator LOW then HIGH and HIGH then LOW around active mtime to observe intermediate compare behavior and confirm no atomicity is assumed.

directed_tests.name: intr_test_sets_state_and_output
directed_tests.intent: Use INTR_TEST to inject each interrupt bit, verify INTR_STATE sets, output follows enable mask, and W1C clears each bit.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_enable_pet_bark_bite
directed_tests.intent: Enable watchdog, verify count increments, pet resets count, bark threshold raises interrupt, and bite threshold asserts alert_o.

directed_tests.name: watchdog_lock_freezes_control_but_allows_pet
directed_tests.intent: Set wd_lock, confirm WATCHDOG_CTRL cannot be cleared or modified except by reset, while WATCHDOG_PET remains functional.

directed_tests.name: reset_clears_lock_and_status
directed_tests.intent: Assert hardware reset after lock/bark/bite activity and verify watchdog lock, counters, interrupt state, and alert return to reset defaults.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads/writes over the valid CSR window with scoreboard checking for legal register behavior, reserved-bit masking, and no bus protocol errors.

random_tests.name: timer_watchdog_constrained_random
random_tests.intent: Randomize prescaler, comparator thresholds, watchdog thresholds, enable/mask/lock sequences, and periodic pet timing to explore mixed timer/watchdog interactions within a short run.

random_tests.name: interrupt_state_random_sequence
random_tests.intent: Randomly exercise INTR_TEST, INTR_ENABLE, and W1C clearing sequences while checking that output pins reflect masked raw state correctly.

risk_areas.reason: Lock is sticky until reset and can easily be implemented incorrectly, especially around write restrictions and pet accessibility.
Non-atomic LOW/HIGH programming can create transient compare conditions and is a common source of off-by-one or glitch bugs.
Specification requires new prescaler value to take effect on the next boundary without resetting the internal prescale counter.
INTR_STATE, INTR_ENABLE, and INTR_TEST interaction is a frequent integration bug and directly affects software-visible behavior.
Bark should raise an interrupt before bite asserts alert_o; threshold equality and escalation ordering need explicit checking.
Upper bits must read as zero and writes must not perturb state; decode mistakes can corrupt adjacent registers.
Reset must clear counters, interrupts, alert state, and lock state; recovery after fatal watchdog conditions is essential.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL protocol compliance under simple traffic
risk_areas.reason: Even with limited budget, basic read/write handshakes, back-to-back accesses, and address alignment should be validated.
